item.title: Defending champions NUST through to Chancellor’s Games semis
item.description: Patience Dube THE National University of Science and Technology (Nust) women’s darts team is through to the semi-finals of the Chancellor of Zimbabwe State Universities Staff Games at Great Zimbabwe University, Masvingo on Thursday. The team that consists of Tariro Mbofana, Brenda Ndlovu, Pretty Ncube, Mirriam Mudzingwa, Sichelesile Moyo, Noxolo Moyo and Candy Sibanda earned themselves a slot in the semi-finals against Lupane State University after winning all their games in the group games on Wednesday. At the competition which began on Sunday with 13 teams drawn into four pools whose group winners progressed to the semis, Nust emerged among the favourites. The Bulawayo ladies darts team who are the defending champions, accounted for Zimbabwe Open University 10-3, they beat Women’s University of Africa 10-2 and ended their group campaign with a 9-6 victory over ZIMCHE in a fixture that was described by Mbofana as tense. We played against ZIMCHE yesterday (Wednesday) and the game was tense because they were also good,” she admitted. “We also played against WUA in the quarter-finals which made us qualify for the semi-finals later today (Thursday),” she said. The ladies are ready to play against Lupane State University in a semi-final match today. University of Zimbabwe, Midlands State University, Bindura University of Science and Education, Chinhoyi University of Technology and Harare Institute of Technology, ZIMCHE, Women University of Africa and Zimbabwe Open University are the institutions taking part. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: RBZ warns digital payments adoption lagging despite record growth @zimsituation
item.description: Source: RBZ warns digital payments adoption lagging despite record growth – herald Business Reporter Zimbabwe’s digital payments ecosystem recorded ZiG1.37 trillion in transaction value and 511, 1 million transactions in the first half of 2026, but the Reserve Bank has warned tha #Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Marvelous Nakamba closes in on European move

item.title: From Nengomasha to Katsande: Kaizer Chiefs eyes Zimbabwean midfielder

item.title: Sandawana confirms 39.9 million-tonne lithium resource from just 30% of mining claim

item.title: More shocking details in Holy Ten’s lula lula scandal: He threatened to kill her then raped her nyoro, 4 accomplices flee
item.description: HARARE – The glittering facade of Zimbabwe’s music industry has been profoundly shaken by the arrest of hip-hop sensation Holy Ten, whose real name is Mukudzei Chitsama, on grave allegations of raping a 14-year-old girl. The incident, which initially unfolded in a shroud of secrecy, has ignited a national conversation about celebrity accountability, the exploitation [...] The post More shocking details in Holy Ten’s lula lula scandal: He threatened to kill her then raped her nyoro, 4 accomplices flee first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Opposition challenges Constitutional Amendment No. 3 in Constitutional Court, seeks to nullify Mnangagwa’s term extension
item.description: OPPOSITION National Constitutional Assembly (NCA) National Executive Committee member Vhurande Mahlupeko has approached the Constitutional Court seeking an order declaring the recently enacted Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act, 2026 null and void. Mahlupeko cited Parliament of Zimbabwe, President Emmerson Mnangagwa, Attorney-General Virginia Mabiza and Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi as [...] The post Opposition challenges Constitutional Amendment No. 3 in Constitutional Court, seeks to nullify Mnangagwa’s term extension appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s rushed mining wager on itself is paying off as it sees a record $2.5 billion profit added to its economy
item.description: Business Insider Africa Zimbabwe’s mineral exports hit a record $2.53 billion in the first half of 2026, an 84% increase over the previous year, driven by higher global demand and commodity prices. The surge is linked to the country’s policy of processing more minerals locally before export, improving value addition and revenue realization. Platinum Group [...] The post Zimbabwe’s rushed mining wager on itself is paying off as it sees a record $2.5 billion profit added to its economy appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Millitary broadcaster faces property seizure after failing to settle employee wages
item.description: THE High Court has ordered the seizure of movable property belonging to Nkululeko Rusununguko TV (NRTV), a media stable owned by the Zimbabwe National Army (ZNA), after it failed to settle debts owed to its employees, which have now ballooned to in excess of US$200,000. The attachment order comes after NRTV failed to pay its [...] The post Millitary broadcaster faces property seizure after failing to settle employee wages appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Police hunt opposition activist Madzibaba veShanduko for ‘undermining police authority’ @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has launched a manhunt for opposition activist Godfrey Karembera, popularly known as “Madzibaba Veshanduko”, over allegations of undermining police authority. In a statement issued Tuesday, national police spokesperson Commissioner Paul Nyathi appealed to members of the public for information that could lead to Karembera’s arrest. “The Zimbabwe Republic Police is [...] The post Police hunt opposition activist Madzibaba veShanduko for ‘undermining police authority’ appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Madhevere and Nyamhuri return for Zimbabwe’s T20I series against India
item.description: CricInfo Tafadzwa Tsiga, the wicketkeeper-batter, has earned his first T20I call-up, while batting allrounder Wessly Madhevere and fast bowler Newman Nyamhuri have been drafted in for Zimbabwe’s three-match T20I series at home against India. The trio has replaced Clive Madande, Tinotenda Maposa and Tashinga Musekiwa from the squad that took on Bangladesh earlier this month. Nyamhuri has recovering from a niggle [...] The post Madhevere and Nyamhuri return for Zimbabwe’s T20I series against India appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: PSL giants avoid each other in Munhumutapa Challenge Cup Round of 32 draw
item.description: Zimbabwe Premier Soccer League heavyweights have avoided facing each other in the Round of 32 of the inaugural ZIFA Munhumutapa Challenge Cup following Wednesday’s draw. The upcoming round will be played on the weekend of 7 to 9 August across the country. Bulawayo giants Highlanders FC will face Blackrock FC, while Dynamos will travel all [...] The post PSL giants avoid each other in Munhumutapa Challenge Cup Round of 32 draw appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Shadowy group calls for cancellation of August concerts ahead of planned anti-CAA3 demonstrations
item.description: A shadowy organisation representing migrants has called on promoters to cancel all music concerts scheduled for August, including highly anticipated shows by Winky D and Jah Prayzah, claiming the events could clash with planned anti-government demonstrations. Calls for protests against the recently enacted Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act have gained traction on social [...] The post Shadowy group calls for cancellation of August concerts ahead of planned anti-CAA3 demonstrations appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: When Period Pain is Normalised: The Hidden Endometriosis Crisis
item.description: When Pain is Normalised: Inside the Hidden Endometriosis Crisis For generations, Black women have been taught to bear severe pelvic pain with quiet dignity, dismissing debilitating cycles as a normal rite of passage. But what happens when "normal period pain" is actually a chronic, destructive warfare raging inside the body? From the medical myths that delay diagnosis to the cultural silence surrounding jeko gono, this article unmasks the reality of endometriosis. Read the powerful stories of advocates Tinevimbo Matambanadzo and Tinopona Katsande, and discover how we are replacing stigma with science, surgery, and support. The post When Period Pain is Normalised: The Hidden Endometriosis Crisis appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Pan-African Parliament begins 7th Parliament session as lawmakers tackle water, trade and governance
item.description: THE First Committee Sittings and Ordinary Session of the Seventh Parliament of the Pan-African Parliament (PAP) are underway in Midrand, South Africa, ahead of the official opening ceremony scheduled for July 27. Permanent Committees have already begun deliberations, with parliamentarians from across Africa meeting to consider key legislative and policy issues before the plenary session. [...] The post Pan-African Parliament begins 7th Parliament session as lawmakers tackle water, trade and governance appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: OPINION | Why Section 328(7) Can’t Stop Mnangagwa from Benefiting Under Amendment No. 3
item.description: By Lucky Ndlovu For months, a loud chorus of politicians, activists, lawyers, and self-appointed constitutional experts has repeated the same claim that Constitution Amendment Act No. 3 (CA3) of 2026 cannot benefit President Emmerson Mnangagwa because of Section 328(7) of the Constitution. The claim has been repeated so often that many now accept it as [...] The post OPINION | Why Section 328(7) Can’t Stop Mnangagwa from Benefiting Under Amendment No. 3 appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News
